description: Smoke test: baseline mode, add-view natural tier. Verify harness runs end-to-end with new prompt_tier/claude_md fields.
observations: FINDING: Agent discovered jig even in baseline mode. Baseline strips jig from prompt but CLAUDE.md still says 'check for matching skills' and .claude/skills/ dirs remain in sandbox. For true no-jig control, need --mode baseline --claude-md none. Score 1.00 — agent completed task correctly.

description: Smoke test: jig mode, add-view natural tier. Compare against baseline.
observations: Jig mode 24% fewer tokens (241K vs 318K), 21% cheaper ($0.65 vs $0.83), 21% faster (66s vs 84s). Both modes scored 1.00. Context field in jig mode provides scenario framing that baseline strips. Harness correctly writes prompt_tier and claude_md fields to results.jsonl.

description: True baseline: --mode baseline --claude-md none. Test if agent uses jig without any nudge.
observations: FINDING: With --claude-md none, agent did NOT use jig (jig_used=false, 0 invocations). Hand-edited successfully. Fastest and cheapest trial (50s, $0.47, 163K tokens). This confirms the 3-level discovery gradient: (1) baseline+none = no jig discovery, (2) baseline+shared = jig discovered via CLAUDE.md nudge, (3) jig+shared = jig used via prompt+nudge. The CLAUDE.md behavioral nudge is the key differentiator for skill discovery, not the prompt mode.

description: Full baseline control: all 7 scenarios, natural tier, --mode baseline --claude-md none, claude-code only.
observations: Overall 0.730. Two scenarios failed: scaffold-test=0.00 (agent never created tests/test_core_service.py), inject-import=0.33 (agent consolidated import into existing line instead of adding standalone line — assertion requires exact jig-style output). Other 5 scenarios scored 1.00. Tier breakdown: easy=0.444, medium=0.889, discovery=1.0, error-recovery=1.0. Greenfield category=0.000 — the hardest for hand-editing. inject-import failure is interesting: agent does semantically correct thing but structurally different from jig output.
